mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue expectations, depressing equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real GDP growth and rising consumer price inflation indicates stagflationary pressure, which forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy while corporate margins compress, yielding negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth < 1.0% and CPIAUCSL monthly change > 0.4%
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the joint Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of peak industrial production, a subsequent monthly decline in the industrial production index signals a supply-side bottleneck reversal and weakening aggregate demand, which historically precedes equity drawdowns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO peaks and then declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns following the trigger condition are not statistically lower than the unconditional mean (p > 0.10), or the signal's hit rate falls below 40% over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding business hiring and economic momentum, which supports corporate earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The rolling 1-year Rank IC of PAYEMS month-over-month changes against 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.02, or positive payrolls months do not yield significantly higher forward returns than negative months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly drop in housing starts below 1.2 million units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which transmits to broader equity markets and depresses forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following the trigger are not significantly negative relative to the baseline distribution, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of retail sales growth and a tightening unemployment rate indicates a self-reinforcing consumption boom that overheats the economy, leading to aggressive monetary tightening expectations and subsequent equity volatility that drags down returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve out-of-sample R-squared by at least 1.5% over a single-series baseline, or forward 60-day returns in the triggered regime are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a sustained rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% compresses equity valuations by increasing the risk-free rate and discounting future cash flows more heavily, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% while CPIAUCSL remains above 3.0% YoY
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns in this regime are not statistically lower than the unconditional mean, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.
